assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 21-25: JQL search returned 1 result showing TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'. Line 7 confirms upstream component is quinn-proto. Line 29: 'Concurrent triage detected on the same upstream component (`quinn-proto`). Another engineer (`engineer-b@example.com`) is actively triaging TC-8019'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 34-48: The warning presented to the engineer includes a table with '| TC-8019   | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com  |' showing both the issue key and assignee.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 44-48: '1. Wait -- pause until the other triage completes, then re-run Step 4.3 to detect any overlap', '2. Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE', '3. Proceed -- create tasks anyway with a `concurrent-triage-overlap` label so the other engineer’s Step 4.3 catches the overlap'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 55: 'The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8.' Also concurrent-triage.md line 53: 'The engineer must choose one of three options before proceeding to Case A/B/C branching in Step 8'. The triage-outcome.md structure places 'Step 7 Gate -- Concurrent Triage Detected' (line 49) before the Case A and Case B sections, and explicitly states the gate must be resolved before remediation proceeds.
